Welcome to Long Horizon Notes. My guest today is Daniel Cho, former bond trader turned index advocate. Daniel, welcome back. Thanks, Nora. Glad to be here. Today's episode is supported by Betterment, automated investing that keeps your fees low and your plan boring. Visit betterment.com/podcast. I want to start with a warning that says well. Here's economist L. D. Serrany from the Talkback in 2016. Leverages feel free until the day it isn't. That line held up. Most blowups I've seen were in bad bets. They were fine bets financed the wrong way. So the risk isn't the asset, it's the borrowing against it. Right. An index fund you own outright is patient. The same fund on margin can be forced to sell at the worst possible moment. We leave the archive there. Daniel, thanks for coming back on. My pleasure, Nora. That's Long Horizon Notes for this week. Take care.
